requirements_scoping.reasoning: Response lists both functional and non-functional requirements with quantified SLAs (99.99% availability, <200ms p95 latency, at-least-once delivery). Assumptions are explicitly stated (MAU, DAU, message sizes, peak factor) but no explicit non-goals are set.

capacity_estimation.reasoning: Derives QPS/storage/bandwidth from assumptions (100B messages/day, 500K DAU, etc.). Computes storage (36.5PB/year raw, 1.8EB/year media) and bandwidth (116GB/s peak media). Numbers drive decisions (need object storage, CDN). Includes sanity checks ('That's huge'), but does not explicitly flag fragile assumptions.

architecture_coherence.reasoning: Every component (WebSocket gateways, Message Service, Kafka, Redis, Cassandra, etc.) is clearly justified against specific requirements or capacity numbers. Diagram and description trace data flow. No hallucinations. Avoids fan-out explosion with group fan-out strategy, which is a specific anti-pattern avoidance.

deep_dive_depth.reasoning: Multiple detailed deep-dives: one-to-one message send (idempotency, ordering, fan-out via pub/sub), group messaging (small vs large with queue-based), presence system (heartbeat, TTL, sweeper). Each has concrete mechanisms. Does not surface non-obvious edge cases like hot key sharding or GC tail latency.

tradeoffs_failure_modes.reasoning: Tradeoffs table covers CAP, push vs pull, ordering, DB choice, group fan-out, presence accuracy. Failure scenarios include MS crash, gateway crash, DB partition, fan-out spikes, duplicates, celebrity bursts, each with mitigations. Acknowledges limits (presence delay, offline pull). No deeper non-obvious failure mode (e.g., thundering herd) explored.
